evals.name: grafana-dashboard-crd-instance-selector
evals.prompt: I'm authoring a GrafanaDashboard CRD for my k3s homelab. I applied the manifest but the dashboard never appears in Grafana. No error shows up when I kubectl describe the GrafanaDashboard. What's wrong and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: The most common cause is an instanceSelector mismatch: the GrafanaDashboard's spec.instanceSelector.matchLabels must match the labels on the Grafana CR itself. The Grafana Operator silently skips CRDs whose instanceSelector doesn't match — no error is emitted to the resource status. Verify with: kubectl get grafana -n monitoring --show-labels, and confirm the label dashboards: grafana is present on the Grafana CR. Then ensure the same matchLabels block appears in the GrafanaDashboard spec.
expectations: Identifies instanceSelector mismatch as the cause, not a generic 'check your YAML' answer
Cites the specific label dashboards: grafana as the homelab standard to match
Provides the exact diagnostic command: kubectl get grafana -n monitoring --show-labels
Explains that the Grafana Operator silently skips non-matching CRDs with no status error
Avoids suggesting Grafana UI edits or restarting the pod as the primary fix

evals.name: prometheus-loki-template-variable-setup
evals.prompt: I want to add a $container template variable to my Grafana dashboard so I can filter both Prometheus panels and Loki log panels by container name. How do I define the variable for each datasource?
evals.expected_output: For Prometheus, use a query variable with label_values(up, container) pointing at the Prometheus datasource (uid: ${DS_PROMETHEUS}). For Loki, the variable definition is different: use a query variable with the Loki datasource (uid: ${DS_LOKI}) and set the query field to {"label": "container", "stream": "{}"} — not label_values(), which is Prometheus-only. Set refresh: 2 on both so options update when the time range changes. In every panel query, include {container="$container"} to respect the variable.
expectations: Uses label_values() syntax for the Prometheus variable and explicitly rejects it for the Loki variable
Specifies the Loki variable query as {"label": "container", "stream": "{}"} (object form, not label_values)
Uses ${DS_PROMETHEUS} and ${DS_LOKI} as the datasource uid references (portable form)
Sets refresh: 2 on the variable definitions so they re-query on time range change
Notes that every panel must include {container="$container"} to avoid confusing mismatches when the dropdown changes

evals.name: sonarr-alert-discord-crd-setup
evals.prompt: I'm setting up Grafana alerting on my k3s homelab. I want an alert to fire when Sonarr has been unreachable for 2 minutes and notify me via Discord. Walk me through the full setup using the Grafana Operator CRD approach.
evals.expected_output: Create a GrafanaFolder CRD first — GrafanaAlertRuleGroup requires a folder to exist before it can be applied. In the GrafanaAlertRuleGroup, define two refIds: refId A with the PromQL expression up{job="sonarr"} pointing at datasourceUid: prometheus (which must match the uid field set explicitly in your GrafanaDatasource), and refId C as a threshold expression using datasourceUid: __expr__ that fires when A < 1. Set for: 2m. For Discord, get the webhook URL from Server Settings → Integrations → Webhooks, store it in a Kubernetes Secret, and reference it via secureSettings in the contact point — never commit the raw URL. Finally, set the default notification policy's contact point to your Discord webhook; without this step, fired alerts go nowhere.
expectations: Requires creating a GrafanaFolder CRD before the GrafanaAlertRuleGroup (prerequisite ordering)
Uses two-refId structure: refId A for the raw PromQL query and refId C with datasourceUid: __expr__ for the threshold condition
Specifies datasourceUid must match the explicit uid field set in the GrafanaDatasource spec (not auto-assigned)
Instructs storing the Discord webhook URL in a Kubernetes Secret and referencing it via secureSettings, not committing it in plain YAML
Calls out that the default notification policy contact point must be set, or fired alerts go nowhere
